description: A multi-domain corpus of policy and regulatory documents collected for the Risk-Aware Policy Clause Importance Ranking project. Documents span legal contracts, privacy policies, academic regulations, and HR/corporate policies.

corpus_stats.note: Legal trimmed to 102 from 510 available CUAD contracts for domain balance

legal.description: Commercial legal contracts sourced from the CUAD dataset (SEC EDGAR filings)

sources.authors: Hendrycks, D., Burns, C., Chen, A., & Ball, S.
sources.paper_title: CUAD: An Expert-Annotated NLP Dataset for Legal Contract Review
sources.access_method: Manual download of CUADv1.json from GitHub release, extracted via extract_cuad_from_json.py
sources.notes: Contracts sourced from SEC EDGAR. Labeled by law students under attorney supervision. Used raw contract text only — our own clause segmentation and risk labeling applied on top. Trimmed to 102 for domain balance.
sources.cite_as: Hendrycks et al. (2021). CUAD: An Expert-Annotated NLP Dataset for Legal Contract Review. arXiv:2103.06268.
sources.bibtex: @article{hendrycks2021cuad, title={CUAD: An Expert-Annotated NLP Dataset for Legal Contract Review}, author={Hendrycks, Dan and Burns, Collin and Chen, Anya and Ball, Spencer}, journal={arXiv preprint arXiv:2103.06268}, year={2021}}

privacy.description: Privacy policies and data protection regulatory texts — mix of HuggingFace dataset and curated regulatory docs

sources.authors: Chalkidis, I., Jana, A., Hartung, D., Bommarito, M., Androutsopoulos, I., Katz, D., & Aletras, N.
sources.paper_title: LexGLUE: A Benchmark Dataset for Legal Language Understanding in English
sources.access_method: HuggingFace datasets library — coastalcph/lex_glue, ledgar config, train split
sources.notes: LEDGAR subset contains legal provisions from SEC-filed contracts. Used for privacy and compliance clause coverage.
sources.cite_as: Chalkidis et al. (2022). LexGLUE: A Benchmark Dataset for Legal Language Understanding in English. ACL 2022.
sources.bibtex: @inproceedings{chalkidis2022lexglue, title={LexGLUE: A Benchmark Dataset for Legal Language Understanding in English}, author={Chalkidis, Ilias and Jana, Abhik and Hartung, Dirk and Bommarito, Michael and Androutsopoulos, Ion and Katz, Daniel Martin and Aletras, Nikolaos}, booktitle={Proceedings of ACL}, year={2022}}

sources.full_name: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) — Curated Policy Clauses
sources.institution: European Parliament and Council of the European Union
sources.access_method: Project-curated — clauses constructed based on GDPR Articles 5, 12, 33, and 44
sources.notes: Covers data retention, right to erasure, breach notification, and cross-border transfer clauses.
sources.cite_as: European Parliament. (2016). Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (General Data Protection Regulation). Official Journal of the European Union, L 119, 1-88.

sources.full_name: California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) — Curated Policy Clauses
sources.access_method: Project-curated — clauses constructed based on Cal. Civ. Code § 1798.100 et seq.
sources.notes: Covers consumer rights, opt-out, data security obligations, and minors' data.
sources.cite_as: California Attorney General. (2018). California Consumer Privacy Act. Cal. Civ. Code § 1798.100 et seq.

sources.access_method: Project-curated based on ePrivacy Directive and ICO cookie guidance
Project-curated based on GDPR employment data guidance

academic.description: University academic integrity policies, student conduct codes, examination regulations, and course policies

sources.full_name: University Policy Web Pages — HTML Scraped
sources.access_method: HTTP scraping of publicly accessible university policy pages
sources.notes: Not all pages successfully scraped due to network restrictions or access controls. Successfully scraped pages saved as domain txt files.

sources.access_method: Project-curated — constructed based on standard university policy frameworks
frameworks_referenced: QAA UK Quality Code for Higher Education
UK Office for Students regulatory guidance
SEVIS (Student and Exchange Visitor Program) compliance requirements
sources.notes: Constructed to cover realistic policy types with natural clause severity variation across Low, Medium, High, and Critical risk levels.

hr.description: Human resources and corporate workplace policy documents covering the full employment lifecycle

sources.full_name: Public HR Guidance Pages — HTML Scraped
sources.access_method: HTTP scraping of publicly accessible HR guidance pages
sources.license: Public — government and professional body web content
sources_attempted: ACAS (Advisory, Conciliation and Arbitration Service) — acas.org.uk
CIPD (Chartered Institute of Personnel and Development) — cipd.org
OPM (U.S. Office of Personnel Management) — opm.gov
SHRM (Society for Human Resource Management) — shrm.org
sources.notes: ACAS pages generally accessible. SHRM and CIPD may require login. Successfully scraped pages saved as domain txt files.

sources.full_name: Curated HR and Corporate Policy Documents
sources.access_method: Project-curated — constructed based on standard HR policy frameworks
frameworks_referenced: SHRM (Society for Human Resource Management) policy templates
CIPD (Chartered Institute of Personnel and Development) guidelines
UK Health and Safety at Work Act 1974
ACAS Code of Practice on Disciplinary and Grievance Procedures
sources.notes: Covers the full employment lifecycle: recruitment, probation, performance, leave, misconduct, grievance, termination, and post-employment obligations. Designed to have realistic risk severity variation across all four risk levels.

notes_for_paper.novelty_claim: To the best of our knowledge, no existing dataset covers policy clause risk severity across legal, privacy, academic, and HR domains simultaneously.
notes_for_paper.labeling_note: Risk labels (Low / Medium / High / Critical) were assigned using a rule-assisted automatic labeler followed by manual verification — see Section 3.2.
notes_for_paper.synthetic_doc_justification: Curated synthetic documents follow established policy frameworks (SHRM, CIPD, QAA, ACAS) and were reviewed for realistic clause severity distribution. This approach is consistent with prior low-resource NLP dataset construction work.
notes_for_paper.domain_balance_note: Legal domain trimmed from 510 to 102 documents to prevent domain imbalance during classifier training.
